creator: michelle@sorbs.net
text: No I haven't - and quite deliberately (mainly because I saw that later and the systems are set to update using freebsd-update and I don't want the kernel to bugger up the patching)...  and I wanted to see if I could get to the bottom of the cause or at least make it reproducible...  I see no reason why zfs on i386 shouldn't work without the need to recompile the kernel (or zfs should be removed so that anyone wishing to use it should have to compile)... and I've finally got progress, single CPU and no panic, multiple CPUs and reproducible panic.. can it be looked at to resolve it..?  (because in reality it has to be some form of bug - ARC should not exhaust the memory, and it should be constrained by the limits in the loader conf(ig))
